# Transcrição: 14-Funnel Activation & Rapid Fire Round #3

- **Fonte:** 14-Funnel Activation & Rapid Fire Round #3
- **Duração:** 05:21
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so when we're in the third phase of our rapid fire testing, we're activating the funnel at this stage. Now I want to take you through two main types of funnels in the coaching industry or the coaching niche. There's really two big ones that you're going to be working with. Now there's different ways to structure those. For example, you could have the funnel starting off with a bot or a lead ad or a normal landing page. But essentially the premise behind it, the structure is essentially the same regardless of what kind of ad format or type of funnel that you choose. So the first thing is to recognize that there's really two main types of funnels. There's high ticket and low ticket. This is based off the price point of the offer of your client. So anything over $2,000 really needs to be sold on a sales call. It's going to be extremely difficult to sell that through inside of a funnel. Now I'm not saying it can't be done, but really the people that are able to sell $2,000 and up offers on in funnels are either selling it to a warm audience or they're extremely good webinar presenters. And so you're kind of hinging your luck here assuming that the person is going to be a great, great webinar presenter. So high ticket funnels typically lead to a sales call. And this is kind of called a strategy call a lot of the times. And then low ticket funnels are anything under $2,000 where you will sell the offer without involving a salesperson. It'll just be through a sales page. The funnel will sell it for you. So here's a typical outline of a high ticket funnel. So we've got the Facebook ad goes to a landing page. People watch a replay or a webinar, either it's automated or not. And then it sends leads to a sales call. And a typical low ticket funnel is a Facebook ad which goes to a landing page or an opt-in. So remember if you had a bot here, this would be a Facebook ad to a bot which would then give the replay, etc. And then instead of the sales call, this one sends them to a sales page. So it sends them straight to the offer. Okay, so this is a version of it more fleshed out. As you can see, there's a lot more work to this kind of funnel than initially meets the eye. So we're starting off with a Facebook ad and this is sending them to a landing page or a bot. In this example, it's a landing page. Once they opt-in, they get sent to a confirmation page. Now you'll notice that we have events on both of those pages.

## [03:01 - 05:21]

And that is so that we can essentially track the bottlenecks track where how people are moving through our funnel. Because we can't always rely on the stats from our landing page software. Once they opt-in, they also get placed into an email sequence. Normally this is a six part email sequence and spaced out one day apart. The email sequence will give them access to the replay, the PDF, whatever that lead magnet is. And there's going to be retargeting as well in place. So the lead magnet replay page, the email sequence and the retargeting are all driving towards the sales page. So you're driving anyone that opted in towards that sales page. And again, we're tracking people that visited the sales page. We've got an add to cart on that as well and then a confirmation. And anyone that didn't purchase and added to cart also gets retargeted. So this is kind of a more fleshed out version of that low ticket funnel. Now again, as I mentioned, you can swap out the landing pages for bots here. In the framework foundations, you will see a whole instructions on how to set up a live training funnel. And that one is using a bot as well as an evergreen funnel. So this is kind of an overview. I'm not going to go too much into the builds of the actual funnels. But just so you know, when you're mapping out the funnels for these kinds of clients, these are the two main funnels, one that will lead to a sales call. And then one that leads to a sales page. And this is kind of an example of the back end of that funnel. So the high ticket one is essentially exactly the same, except instead of sending them to a sales page, it sends them to a call. And then on the back of that, there's a calendar system and a confirmation system. And so this is how to pick the funnel for your clients. It's going to be based off the price point. At this stage, it's now time to activate that funnel by choosing a funnel, starting to build it out and then rolling out the third round of rapid fire testing, which is testing the creative.
